# KinMate v1.0.0 — Foundation release

## Release Date

2026-06-06

## Summary

v1.0 is the launch foundation of KinMate — the moment the public site,
the in-app paywall, and the subscription model all started speaking
the same language. The product itself goes much further; for the
complete user-facing story, see the current release notes:
[v1.2.0 — The Health Intelligence release](./v1.2.0.md).

This note exists for archive purposes only.

## What v1.0 set in stone

**A 60-day free Pro trial for every new account.** Long enough to
file last year's reports, set up reminders for everyone you care for,
and try a few AI interpretations before you decide whether to pay.

**A flexible-seat-pool subscription model.** You don't pay for empty
seats. Every paid tier comes with 2 managed seats included; family
plans add a flexible pool you can spend on real family members or
extra dependents — your choice, not ours.

**The Family 8 lifetime founder offer.** Launched at $199.99
(regular $399.99) as a one-time purchase. Yours forever, including
all future core feature updates. Honored beyond the launch window.

**Aligned messaging.** Pricing copy on the public site, in the in-app
paywall, and in the legal terms all reference the same numbers and
rules — no mismatch, no surprises at checkout.

## What stays true in every release after v1.0

- The 60-day trial.
- The flexible seat model.
- The Family 8 lifetime tier for purchases made during the launch
  window.
- The privacy posture: local-first by default, bring-your-own cloud
  for backup, end-to-end encrypted when sync is necessary, zero
  analytics SDKs.

## Where the actual product story lives now

See [v1.2.0](./v1.2.0.md). v1.2 added the Health Intelligence layer
(AI report interpretation, bilingual one-tap translation, health
trends, genetic risk, medical case management, emergency cards,
patient summary, family dashboard) — these are the features a real
user will install KinMate for.

## Notes for operators

- This site release note should stay in sync with the corresponding
  app release note for the same version. If pricing rules change
  later, update both repositories together.
